---
title: "S3: Hyperspheres, Quaternions, And Hopf Coils"
---

S3 pages are scaffolded for finite hypersphere, quaternion, Hopf, and spin chapters.

S3 is where the book moves from circular repetition to higher-dimensional phase and rotation structure. The practical reading order is: first learn the finite hypersphere scaffold, then the quaternion algebra seed, then the bounded Hopf coordinate pages, and only then the spin/sign ambiguity material.

::: {.learning-unit}

## What To Learn Here

The S3 unit should teach why a hidden phase can matter. In the bounded Hopf pages, a visible coordinate can share a state with an extra circular phase. In the quaternion pages, rotation-like algebra is checked in a finite proof spine before any broad geometric claim is made.

:::

<div class="warning-box">
This section separates checked coordinate and algebra seeds from full topology, SO(3), robotics, and fibration claims. Treat a claim as proved only through the generated theorem cards.
</div>

## Lesson Path

1. [Hyperspheres](hyperspheres.qmd): finite suspension counts and S3 scaffold vocabulary.
2. [Quaternions](quaternions.qmd): the checked algebra seed for real-quaternion coordinates.
3. [Hopf Coils](hopf_coils.qmd): bounded coordinate and phase-action facts.

::: {.checkpoint-box}

## Checkpoint

When reading an S3 page, identify whether the claim is about a checked coordinate/algebra seed or about future full topology.

:::

## Source Trail

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="S3Q-T0001"></div>
<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="S3Q-0001"></div>

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
